---
title: "County-level education characteristics & Public Assistance Program Support"
---

County-level relationships between student enrollment, share in poverty, and school funding gaps with federal safety net programs (dollars, and as a share of a county's tradable income). Data: NCES School District Finance Survey (2023), Manduca 2025, BEA Personal Income by County (2022), District Cost Database v6.0 (2023).

## Distributions

![Distribution of student enrollment in counties.](enrollment_histogram.png)

![Distribution of county-level Medicaid spending.](medicaid_spending_histogram.png)

## Medicaid

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Dollars

![Student enrollment vs. Medicaid dollars.](enrollment_vs_pub_asst_med_val.png)

![County student poverty rate vs. Medicaid dollars.](medicaid_val_vs_pov.png)

![Average school funding gap vs. Medicaid dollars.](medicaid_val_vs_fundinggap.png)

### Share of Tradable Income

![Student enrollment vs. Medicaid as a share of tradable income.](enrollment_vs_medicaid_pct_tra.png)

![County student poverty rate vs. Medicaid as a share of tradable income.](medicaid_pct_vs_pov.png)

![Average school funding gap vs. Medicaid as a share of tradable income.](medicaid_pct_vs_fundinggap.png)

:::

## SNAP

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Dollars

![Student enrollment vs. SNAP dollars.](enrollment_vs_snap_val.png)

### Share of Tradable Income

![Student enrollment vs. SNAP as a share of tradable income.](enrollment_vs_snap_pct_tra.png)
